data.title: Balanced Risk-Reward Amid Clinical Uncertainty: Maintaining Hold on Mereo BioPharma
data.description:
LifeSci Capital analyst Kate Dellorusso has maintained their neutral stance on MREO stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kate Dellorusso has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both clinical uncertainty and financial positioning. The recent Phase 3 trials of setrusumab in osteogenesis imperfecta failed to achieve their primary fracture-rate endpoint, and while younger patients showed encouraging signals on vertebral fractures and pain, the ultimate regulatory and commercial path for the program remains unclear, leading the team to adopt a wait-and-see stance as additional analyses continue.

Mereo’s broader pipeline offers potential upside, including efforts to secure a partner for alvelestat and the planned Phase 2 trial of vanticumab by āshibio, but these value drivers are still in early or partnered stages and carry execution risk. At the same time, the company’s cash balance, reduced operating expenses, and runway into mid-2027 provide some support, yet not enough de-risking to warrant a more constructive view, so the risk‑reward profile appears balanced and supports maintaining a Market Perform (Hold) rating.

According to TipRanks, Dellorusso is an analyst with an average return of -27.8% and a 10.00% success rate. Dellorusso covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Upstream Bio, Inc., Soleno Therapeutics, and Crinetics Pharmaceuticals.

In another report released today, TipRanks – OpenAI also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $0.34 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: SECO: Strengthening Earnings, Strategic Software Growth, and Upcoming Catalysts Support Buy Rating
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Arianna Terazzi from Intesa Sanpaolo maintained a Buy rating on SECO S.p.A., with a price target of €3.70.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Arianna Terazzi has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including SECO’s strengthening earnings profile and improving balance sheet. She expects an ongoing recovery in profitability, supported by solid top-line growth in edge computing, expanding volumes in hardware, and better cash generation that should keep leverage around just over 1x EBITDA, thus preserving financial flexibility.

Terazzi also highlights the strategic relevance of CLEA’s recurring software revenues and the visible pipeline of new design wins, most of which come from newly acquired customers and are set to scale from 2026 onward. In her view, upcoming disclosures on the commercial backlog and near-term revenue guidance could act as catalysts, supporting the investment case and justifying a positive stance on the stock.

According to TipRanks, Terazzi is ranked #2854 out of 12068 analysts.

data.title: Intuitive Surgical: Stapler Reload Recall Seen as Contained, Buy Rating Reaffirmed
data.description:
William Blair analyst Brandon Vazquez has maintained their bullish stance on ISRG stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brandon Vazquez has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that, in his view, limit the financial and commercial impact of the stapler reload recall. He emphasizes that the issue is confined to specific 8 mm SureForm 30 gray reloads rather than the stapling systems themselves, which helps contain the scope of the problem and reduces risks to the broader da Vinci franchise.

He also notes that Intuitive has substitute reload options available so surgeons can continue procedures with minimal interruption, and that the gray reloads represented a relatively small share of overall usage before the recall. Taken together, these points lead him to conclude that the recall, while serious from a safety perspective, is unlikely to materially affect Intuitive Surgical’s business trajectory or his positive investment thesis on ISRG.

Vazquez covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Intuitive Surgical, Dexcom, and Henry Schein. According to TipRanks, Vazquez has an average return of -5.7% and a 27.87% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Autodesk: Early Margin Expansion and Stabilizing Demand Support Buy Rating
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Elizabeth Porter from Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on Autodesk, with a price target of $350.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Elizabeth Porter has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Autodesk’s resilience and earnings power. While the Architecture Billings Index still signals a contracting environment, the month‑over‑month improvement in billings, inquiries, and design contracts points to early signs of stabilization rather than continued deterioration.

At the company level, Porter highlights that Autodesk delivered another robust Q4, with consistent execution and FY27 guidance that supports upward estimate revisions. She views the firm as being in the early stages of a margin expansion cycle, with several levers to protect and grow profitability even amid soft end‑market demand and macro uncertainty, which underpins her favorable risk‑reward and Buy recommendation.

In another report released on March 4, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $331.00 price target.
